title: physocarpus opulifolius var. tomentellus
intro: Physocarpus opulifolius var. tomentellus, commonly known as the Eastern Ninebark, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of saponins and other compounds. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
